stage_name: Convergence and Final Peer Set Selection

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Auto-include: Q1 on FEAUM (primary value driver, rho=0.85 vs P/FRE). Largest alt manager globally ($1.275T AUM); defines the upper bound of scale premium in the universe. Q1 on DE/share. 5 dedicated sources, 75% metric coverage. Strategically instructive as the benchmark case for perpetual capital scaling and wealth distribution.

Auto-include: Q1 on FEAUM. Q1 on DE/share and highest FRE_Margin in universe (Q1). Highest metric coverage (80%) among the Q1 cohort. Strategically instructive for origination-platform model and insurance-channel integration — directly relevant to PAX's credit platform buildout.

Auto-include: Q1 on FEAUM. Q2 on DE/share, Q2 on FRE_Growth. 4 sources, 60% coverage (meets minimum). Distinctive asset-light carve-out from Brookfield Corporation; demonstrates capital-light fee-stream model and perpetual capital approach from a non-US/EM-exposed platform — relevant to PAX's Latam positioning.

Q2 on FEAUM; Q1 on FRE_Growth (fastest-growing among large peers) and Q2 on DE/share. 5 sources, 75% metric coverage. Strategically instructive for capital-markets platform diversification and insurance balance-sheet integration (Global Atlantic). Highest FRE growth rate among the Q1-Q2 FEAUM cohort, making it the aspirational growth benchmark.

Q2 on FEAUM; Q1 on DE/share (strongest DE/share among Q2 FEAUM firms). Q2 on FRE_Growth. 4 sources, 70% metric coverage. Best-in-cohort on credit-oriented multi-asset model; demonstrates how a credit-first manager commands premium DE/share multiples. Directly instructive for PAX's credit platform launch thesis.

Q2 on FEAUM; Q2 on DE/share; Q1 on FRE_Growth. 4 sources, 70% metric coverage. Instructive as a turnaround/restructuring case: demonstrates how a firm can recover DE/share momentum and FRE growth after a period of strategic drift. Valuable cautionary-and-recovery archetype for governance playbook.

Q2 on FEAUM but Q4 on DE/share — included as cautionary case illustrating the disconnect between scale and per-share earnings when management fee rates are high and permanent capital vehicles are over-leveraged. Q2 on FRE_Growth indicates strong fundraising momentum offsetting poor DE conversion. 4 sources, 65% metric coverage.

Q3 on FEAUM — same scale cohort as PAX. Q3 on DE/share but Q1 on FRE_Growth (strongest growth in the Q3 FEAUM tier). 3 sources, 70% metric coverage. Critical aspirational benchmark within PAX's own size tier: demonstrates what FRE growth acceleration looks like for a manager transitioning from sub-scale to scale. Most instructive analog for PAX's 3-5 year trajectory.

Q3 on FEAUM — PAX's direct scale cohort. Highest metric coverage in the universe (100%). 3 sources. Secondary/solutions specialist with differentiated model (GP-led secondaries, co-investments) that illustrates the fee-multiple trade-offs of non-flagship product lines. Useful as a scale-comparable with distinct strategic positioning. Q3 DE/share aligns with PAX's current positioning.

Q2 on FEAUM; 100% metric coverage (tied with STEP for highest in universe). 4 sources. European-listed PE manager; provides geographic and structural diversity in the peer set. Instructive for ESG integration as a valuation narrative and for the listed partnership structure in non-US markets. Absence of DE/share data limits driver-level comparisons but quantitative completeness is high.

4 sources, 65% metric coverage. Quartile data unavailable on primary drivers due to incomplete DE/FEAUM comparability under IFRS. Included for qualitative strategic instructiveness: Partners Group operates the most institutionally advanced global wealth-distribution model in the peer set (integrated solutions, discretionary mandates), directly relevant to PAX's ambitions to expand beyond Latam institutional LPs. Provides geographic and listing-structure diversity.

excluded_candidates.exclusion_rationale: Target company — not included in peer set by definition.

Fails metric coverage threshold: 40% (minimum required: 60%). Q1 FEAUM but balance-sheet investor structure (BN consolidates BAM, renewable power, infrastructure, real estate at corporate level) makes like-for-like multiple comparison misleading. BAM (asset-light fee entity) already represents the Brookfield family in the peer set.

Meets source (3) and coverage (70%) thresholds but has no quartile data on FEAUM, DE/share, or FRE_Growth — insufficient quantitative driver comparability. Secondaries/solutions model diverges significantly from PAX's primary direct-investing model. STEP covers similar strategic territory with superior coverage (100%) and full quartile data.
Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources in source_catalog.json. 3 strategic actions documented but insufficient primary evidence for quantitative validation.

Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. Q4 on FEAUM and Q3 on DE/share; would qualify as cautionary case on scale but lacks the source coverage required for defensible strategic action analysis.

Meets source (3) and coverage (75%) thresholds but has no quartile data on primary value drivers (FEAUM, DE/share) due to structural differences under UK GAAP reporting. European credit specialist; ICP's strategic profile is more closely aligned with European insurance-linked credit than with Latam alt management. Insufficient strategic instructiveness for PAX playbook.

Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. Q4 on DE/share. Quant/systematic hedge fund model is structurally distinct from the fundamental PE/infra/credit alt management model that drives PAX comparables.

Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. 60% metric coverage (barely meets threshold) but only 3 strategic actions. Recently listed (2024); insufficient track record as a public company for meaningful valuation driver comparisons.
Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources in source_catalog.json. 60% metric coverage, 4 strategic actions — borderline qualitatively but insufficient primary documentation.

Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. Q4 on FEAUM and Q4 on DE/share and Q4 on FRE_Growth — would serve as cautionary case but lack of primary sources makes strategic action interpretation unreliable. Only 2 documented actions (lowest in the universe).
Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. Q4 on FRE_Growth. French holding structure (SCA) creates significant GAAP comparability issues for multiple-based analysis.

Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. Fails coverage threshold: 45% (minimum 60%). Q4 on FEAUM, Q4 on DE/share, Q4 on FRE_Growth. While Brazil-domiciled and therefore a direct regional peer to PAX, the data deficiency makes inclusion indefensible. Flagged as a gap for future runs.

Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. Fails coverage threshold: 35%. Structural outlier: balance-sheet investor (not fee-earning alt manager) — the Q4 FEAUM and outlier EV/FEAUM ratio (571%) are definitional artifacts, not valuation comparables. Already flagged as structural outlier in correlations.json (COR-008 sensitivity analysis).

Fails minimum source requirement: 0 dedicated sources. Q4 on FEAUM and Q4 on FRE_Growth. French conglomerate structure (publicly listed but hybrid balance-sheet/manager model) limits comparability with pure-play alt managers.

flagged_gaps: VINP (Vinci Compass / Vinci Partners): Brazil-domiciled alt manager is the single most relevant regional analog to PAX (shared Latam market exposure, similar AUM scale) but fails both source and coverage thresholds. Data collection for a future run should prioritize VINP CVM filings, earnings releases, and investor presentations to enable inclusion.
PHLL excluded pre-screening: delisted December 2025, zero public data, zero strategic actions. No path to inclusion.

BN (Brookfield Corporation): Q1 FEAUM but only 40% metric coverage and hybrid balance-sheet structure. BAM (the asset-light fee carve-out) is the appropriate Brookfield representative; BN's exclusion is structural, not evidentiary.

No firm formally meets stable_value_driver criteria (requires abs(rho)>=0.5 on BOTH primary multiples with N>=12). FEAUM qualifies under leave-one-out sensitivity (excluding 3i outlier, rho=0.81 vs EV/FEAUM, N=13). Final peer set reflects this high-confidence empirical signal despite the formal classification gap.
FRE_Growth_YoY quartile data is absent for STEP (FIRM-011) and EQT (FIRM-015) due to insufficient coverage in the standardized dataset. This limits growth-trajectory comparisons for those two firms.
selection_methodology: Two-pass selection. Pass 1 applied eligibility filters: minimum 2 dedicated sources in source_catalog.json AND minimum 60% metric coverage in quantitative_data.json, reducing the 25-firm candidate universe to 13 eligible firms. Pass 2 applied positive-inclusion rules: (a) auto-include all firms in Q1 on FEAUM (the primary value driver, rho=0.85 vs P/FRE) that pass eligibility — yielding BX, APO, BAM (BN excluded by coverage); (b) include Q2 FEAUM firms with the highest strategic instructiveness across DE/share and FRE_Growth dimensions — yielding KKR, ARES, CG; (c) include OWL as a cautionary case (Q2 FEAUM, Q4 DE/share) illustrating scale-without-earnings-efficiency; (d) include PAX's scale-cohort comparables (Q3 FEAUM: TPG, STEP) as aspirational near-term benchmarks; (e) include EQT and Partners Group for geographic and structural diversity (European-listed managers with 4 sources and strong coverage). Final set of 11 firms spans Q1-Q3 on FEAUM, covers 3 exchanges (NYSE, NASDAQ, SIX/Stockholm), and provides sufficient driver variation for regression and playbook synthesis. HLNE and ICP were considered but excluded due to insufficient primary-driver quartile data and strategic overlap with already-included peers.
